title: BENFOTIAMINE
description: Benfotiamine is a synthetic, fat-soluble derivative of thiamine (vitamin B1) that exhibits significantly higher bioavailability and tissue penetration than standard water-soluble thiamine. It works by activating the enzyme transketolase to inhibit harmful metabolic pathways triggered by high glucose, and it is primarily used to treat diabetic neuropathy and age-related cognitive decline.

research.title: Benfotiamine and Cognitive Decline in Alzheimer's Disease: Results of a Randomized Placebo-Controlled Phase IIa Clinical Trial
research.summary: This clinical trial found that benfotiamine significantly slowed cognitive decline and improved brain glucose metabolism in patients with mild Alzheimer's disease over 12 months.

research.title: BOND study: a randomised double-blind, placebo-controlled trial over 12 months to assess the effects of benfotiamine on morphometric, neurophysiological and clinical measures in patients with type 2 diabetes with symptomatic polyneuropathy
research.summary: This study evaluated whether long-term benfotiamine supplementation could improve nerve function and clinical symptoms in diabetic patients with nerve damage.

research.title: Advances in the management of diabetic neuropathy
research.summary: A review highlighting benfotiamine's ability to inhibit damaging glucose-driven pathways, making it a key pathogenetically oriented treatment for neuropathic complications.

research.title: Benfotiamine in diabetic polyneuropathy (BENDIP): results of a randomised, double blind, placebo-controlled clinical study
research.summary: Clinical results showed that higher doses of benfotiamine led to significant improvements in neuropathy pain scores compared to placebo.

research.title: A double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial on benfotiamine treatment in patients with diabetic nephropathy
research.summary: Research investigating the potential for benfotiamine to protect kidney function by reducing the production of advanced glycation end products (AGEs).

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly report success using benfotiamine for nerve pain and neuropathy, often describing it as more effective than standard B-vitamins with very few side effects.

effects.label: Superior Bioavailability:
effects.description: Highly effective compared to thiamine HCl because its fat-soluble nature allows it to cross cell membranes easily.

effects.label: Neuropathy Pain:
effects.description: Consistently rated as an effective intervention for diabetic and alcoholic nerve damage in both clinical trials and user reports.

effects.label: Alcohol Recovery:
effects.description: Used effectively to replenish thiamine levels in those recovering from severe alcohol dependence.

effects.label: Dose-Dependent:
effects.description: Effectiveness often increases with higher dosages, particularly for chronic nerve pain.

dosage.label: Standard Therapeutic Dose:
dosage.description: Often taken at 300mg to 600mg daily for neuropathy management.

dosage.label: Alzheimer's Protocols:
dosage.description: Clinical trials have utilized 300mg twice daily (600mg total) for cognitive benefits.

dosage.label: Advanced Stacking:
dosage.description: Users often combine 150-300mg with Alpha-Lipoic Acid for synergistic nerve protection.

sideEffects.label: Low Toxicity:
sideEffects.description: Generally reported to have a very high safety profile with minimal side effects even at high doses.

sideEffects.label: Mild GI Distress:
sideEffects.description: Occasional reports of upset stomach if taken on an empty stomach.

sideEffects.label: Skin Rash:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of allergic skin reactions or 'vitamin smells' in sweat at extremely high doses.

availability.label: Sourcing Difficulty:
availability.description: Users report that high-quality, pure benfotiamine can be harder to find than standard B1, often appearing in 'shady' or niche brands.

availability.label: Supplement Status:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter dietary supplement in the US and Europe.
